chapter_frames.purpose: Introduce the learning question, season promise, reader benefit and scope boundary.
Explain origin, vocabulary and why the concept matters.
Teach the basic structure or mechanism in simple but disciplined form.
Show how the topic is known, sourced, debated or verified.
Translate the concept into an example, public system, historical case or practical scenario.
Use diagrams, tables, concept maps, timelines or examples to improve understanding.
Explain how readers can apply the concept or interpret related real-world situations.
Record caveats, competing views, limitations and misuse risks.

chapter_frames.chapter_role: Comparison with adjacent ideas or systems
chapter_frames.purpose: Compare with related concepts, schools, methods, systems or interpretations.
Discuss what is changing, unresolved or likely to matter next.
Help the reader consolidate what was learned and reflect on its meaning.
Summarise the season and prepare continuation, archive, or adjacent series planning.
